formations_blocks: Haiti sitting deeper and then pressing after the goal
adjustments: Morocco using short corner and short throw routines to create a chance
key_matchups: Duverne vs Morocco's back line on the crossing move for the goal
Joseph vs Bounou on the finish
Bellegarde vs the Morocco goal from distance
phases.summary: Morocco started on top, keeping most of the ball through midfield and creating the first dangerous box combination, while Haiti initially threatened from a free kick. Haiti then struck first through Duverne’s run and cross for Joseph’s flick past Bounou, after which they maintained pressure and forced Morocco to respond, including a set-piece move that ended with Saibari missing high.

formations_blocks: Haiti in a defensive block after scoring early, looking to counterattack
Morocco pushing forward and attacking with long balls over the top
Open, frenetic transitional game rather than settled possession
adjustments: Morocco increasingly using long balls over the top to El Kaabi on the right flank
Hakimi advancing from deep to join the attack
Saibari making aggressive box runs and becoming Morocco's main attacking spark
key_matchups: Placide vs Hakimi and El Kaabi
phases.summary: After Haiti’s early goal, the match shifted into a lively transitional phase with Morocco taking more of the initiative and trying to stretch Haiti’s backline using direct balls and forward runs. Haiti, meanwhile, appeared content to absorb pressure and threaten on the counter, with Johny Placide repeatedly denying Morocco’s best chances as the game opened up.

adjustments: Half-time message from Mohamed Ouahbi appeared to emphasize patience
key_matchups: Wilson Isidor striking with his right foot while keeping his left foot planted
phases.summary: The brief halftime notes suggest Morocco were instructed to stay patient, which is reflected in their repeated use of short-corner routines after the restart. The only clear individual technical detail highlighted is Wilson Isidor’s powerful right-footed goal strike, while the scoreboard at the break stands level at 2-2.

formations_blocks: slower, more open second-half phase with both sides struggling to retain possession
Morocco in a transition-heavy attacking shape, repeatedly breaking quickly after turnovers
Haiti defending in a compact/reactive block at times, then countering when they win the ball
adjustments: no changes from either side at half-time
match tempo dropped significantly after the break
Haiti attempted to counter more directly through Joseph after regains
Morocco responded to Haiti's free-kick with an immediate break
key_matchups: Saibari vs Haiti's back line on the cross from El Kaabi
Joseph vs Morocco's recovery pressure in transition
phases.summary: The second half began with no structural changes, but the game’s rhythm shifted sharply as the pace slowed and both teams became sloppier in possession. Morocco remained the more threatening side, creating chances through runs in behind and shots from distance, while Haiti’s best moments came from winning the ball and trying to break quickly, though their transitions were often indecisive. The phase also featured a dangerous set-piece moment for Haiti and an immediate Moroccan counter, underlining how turnover-heavy and unstable the contest had become.

formations_blocks: Haiti counterattacking shape / defending under pressure
Morocco pressing after turnovers in midfield
adjustments: Hakimi taking a free kick from 26 yards as a direct set-piece threat
Morocco substitutions: Rahimi on as a decisive attacking change, El Mourabet for El Aynaoui, Mazraoui for Salah-Eddine
Haiti substitutions: Arcus for Duverne, Simon for Jean Jacques, Pierrot for Joseph
key_matchups: Hakimi vs Haiti wall/defenders on the free kick
Rahimi vs Haiti defense on the corner-led goal
Morocco midfield interceptions vs Haiti midfield/passing lanes
phases.summary: Morocco controlled the closing stages with patience and composure, repeatedly using pressure, turnovers and set pieces to pin Haiti back. The breakthrough came from substitute Soufiane Rahimi after a corner, and Morocco then looked capable of extending the lead as Haiti’s counterattacks were increasingly blunted by Morocco’s defensive recoveries.
